title: C4-MT017-QuickSwitcher

scope.summary:
Implement the Quick Switcher overlay for the native Handshake shell. The switcher opens on Mod-P (defaultChord from APP_KEYBINDING_ACTIONS, app.quick_switcher.open) or via GO > Quick Switcher from the top menu bar (MT-015). It is a full live-search overlay over the Loom graph (documents, blocks, symbols, work packets, microtasks, user-manual pages, wiki pages, files, tag hubs) backed by the real PostgreSQL backend via GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search. Results are sorted with recently-visited items first, using the backend recents store (GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents). Selecting a result records the visit (POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents) and navigates the active pane to the target.

This is a direct port of app/src/components/QuickSwitcher.tsx. The React version uses async useEffect debounced search; the native version uses a tokio::spawn background task (or the existing async-bridge pattern used in earlier MTs) with a 150 ms debounce to match the React timer.

Goal and expected diff shape: create src/frontend/overlays/quick_switcher.rs (~350-450 lines), update src/frontend/overlays/mod.rs (pub mod quick_switcher), update src/frontend/app.rs to open/close the switcher and dispatch QuickSwitcherAction.

Files to create or edit:
- CREATE src/frontend/overlays/quick_switcher.rs
- EDIT   src/frontend/overlays/mod.rs — pub mod quick_switcher
- EDIT   src/frontend/app.rs — keymap binding, open/close flag, dispatch QuickSwitcherAction

SOURCE KINDS TO SEARCH (exact list from QuickSwitcher.tsx QUICK_SWITCHER_SOURCE_KINDS):
  loom_block | file | tag_hub | document | symbol | work_packet | micro_task | user_manual_page | wiki_page
Pass as source_kinds query param to GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search?q=...&source_kinds=loom_block,file,...&limit=25.

BACKEND DATA FLOW:
1. On open: call GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents?limit=20 and store the returned hit_key list for ordering (port of listQuickSwitcherRecents).
2. On query change (debounced 150 ms): if query.trim() is non-empty and workspace_id is set, spawn async GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search?q={query}&source_kinds=...&limit=25 (port of searchLoomGraph). Store results in QuickSwitcherState.results.
3. orderedResults: sort results placing any hit whose hit_key (= "{source_kind}:{ref_id}") appears in recents first, by recents order; port of the React useMemo orderedResults sort.
4. On result selected: call POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents with { result_kind, source_kind, ref_id, title, excerpt, metadata } (port of recordQuickSwitcherRecent), update local recents list, then dispatch navigation.

RUST ASYNC PATTERN: reuse whatever async-to-egui bridge pattern was established in earlier MTs (e.g. a tokio channel with Arc<Mutex<Option<T>>> results cell, or a poll-on-frame approach). The bridge must NOT block the egui frame. Debounce: store last_query_sent and a pending_since: Option<Instant>; only fire the search if now - pending_since >= 150ms and last_query_sent != current_query.

QUICKSWITCHER STATE STRUCT:
  pub struct QuickSwitcherState {
    pub open: bool,
    pub query: String,
    pub results: Vec<LoomGraphSearchHit>,
    pub recents: Vec<String>,  // hit_key list
    pub loading: bool,
    pub error: Option<String>,
    pub recents_error: Option<String>,
    pub selected_index: usize,
    pub first_open: bool,  // for focus management
    pub pending_search_at: Option<std::time::Instant>,
    pub last_query_sent: String,
  }

LOOM GRAPH SEARCH HIT STRUCT (Rust equivalent of LoomGraphSearchHit from api.ts):
  pub struct LoomGraphSearchHit {
    pub source_kind: String,  // "loom_block" | "file" | "tag_hub" | "document" | "symbol" | "work_packet" | "micro_task" | "user_manual_page" | "wiki_page"
    pub result_kind: String,
    pub ref_id: String,
    pub title: String,
    pub excerpt: Option<String>,
    pub metadata: serde_json::Value,
  }
  fn hit_key(hit: &LoomGraphSearchHit) -> String { format!("{}:{}", hit.source_kind, hit.ref_id) }

SOURCE_LABELS (port of SOURCE_LABELS from QuickSwitcher.tsx):
  fn source_label(kind: &str) -> &str { match kind { "loom_block" => "Loom Block", "file" => "File", "tag_hub" => "Tag Hub", "document" => "Document", "symbol" => "Symbol", "work_packet" => "Work Packet", "micro_task" => "Microtask", "user_manual_page" => "UserManual Page", "wiki_page" => "Wiki Page", _ => "Unknown" } }

EGUI OVERLAY LAYOUT (port of QuickSwitcher.tsx render):
1. egui::Window with title_bar(false), anchor(CENTER_CENTER), fixed_size([600, 460]), always_on_top, collapsible(false).
2. Header: eyebrow label "Open", h3 "Quick Switcher", Close button on right.
3. Search input: text_edit_singleline with placeholder "Open by title, block, symbol, WP, MT, manual, or wiki page" — request focus on first_open.
4. Status row (aria-live equivalent: just a label): if query is empty → "Type to search the project graph"; if no workspace → "No workspace selected"; if loading → "Searching graph..."; if error → show error; else → "{N} result(s)" + optional recents_error note.
5. Results list: egui::ScrollArea::vertical, iterate ordered_results, each row is a selectable button with:
   - kind chip (source_label, small muted font)
   - title (bold)
   - excerpt (muted, truncated to ~80 chars)
   - target label (what opening it does, computed by open_target_for_hit)
   Selected row highlighted. Disabled if openTargetForHit returns enabled=false.
6. Keyboard: ArrowDown/Up move selection, Enter selects, Escape closes (port of QuickSwitcher.tsx onKeyDown).
7. Backdrop: full-screen Area behind window; click closes.

open_target_for_hit (port of openTargetForLoomSearchHit from loom_search_open_target.ts):
  pub enum QuickSwitcherTarget { UserManual { slug: String }, WikiPage { projection_id: String }, Document { document_id: String }, LoomBlock { block_id: String }, CodeSymbol { symbol_entity_id: String }, WorkPacket { wp_id: String }, MicroTask { mt_id: String, wp_id: Option<String> }, Unsupported }
  Map source_kind + metadata fields to the appropriate variant; Unsupported disables the row.

NAVIGATION DISPATCH in app.rs:
  match target {
    UserManual { slug } => navigate active pane to user-manual tab + set focus_slug,
    WikiPage { projection_id } => navigate to loom-wiki-page tab + set focus_id,
    Document { document_id } => open document in active pane (openDocuments push),
    LoomBlock { block_id } => navigate to loom-block tab + set focus_id,
    CodeSymbol { symbol_entity_id } => navigate to code-symbol tab + set focus_id,
    WorkPacket { wp_id } => navigate to kernel-dcc tab + set wp focus,
    MicroTask { mt_id, wp_id } => navigate to kernel-dcc tab + set mt+wp focus,
    Unsupported => log warning, no navigation,
  }

ACCESSKIT:
- Window root: role=Dialog, modal=true, label="Quick switcher", author_id=0xC4_0017_0001.
- Search input: role=SearchBox, label="Quick switcher search", author_id=0xC4_0017_0002.
- Status label: role=Live (or Label with live_atomic), author_id=0xC4_0017_0003.
- Results list: role=ListBox, label="Quick switcher results", author_id=0xC4_0017_0004.
- Each result row: role=Option, label=title, selected=is_selected, disabled=!enabled, author_id=0xC4_0017_0100 + row_index (capped at 25 rows).

TESTS:
1. Unit: hit_key() produces "{source_kind}:{ref_id}" correctly.
2. Unit: ordered_results sorts recents-matching hits to the front in recents-list order.
3. Unit: source_label returns correct string for all 9 source kinds.
4. kittest (mock async): open switcher, type "test", assert loading=true within 150ms, inject mock results, assert list renders with correct title and kind chip.
5. kittest: press Escape, assert switcher closed.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Pressing Mod-P (or the configured chord from WorkspaceSettings keybindings["app.quick_switcher.open"]) opens the quick switcher overlay centred on screen.
AC2: The search input receives focus automatically on open.

AC3: Typing a query and waiting 150 ms fires GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search with source_kinds=loom_block,file,tag_hub,document,symbol,work_packet,micro_task,user_manual_page,wiki_page and limit=25 against the real PostgreSQL backend.
AC4: The status row shows 'Searching graph...' during the in-flight request and then '{N} result(s)' when results arrive.
AC5: Results are sorted with any hit whose hit_key appears in the recents list ranked first (by recents-list order).
AC6: Selecting a 'document' result opens that document in the active pane (adds to openDocuments and sets activeDocumentId).
AC7: Selecting a 'work_packet' result navigates the active pane to the kernel-dcc tab and sets the WP focus.
AC8: Selecting any result calls POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents and the returned hit_key is prepended to the local recents list.
AC9: Pressing Escape closes the switcher without navigating.
AC10: Rows for source kinds with no supported open target are rendered with disabled styling and cannot be activated.
AC11: AccessKit tree has Dialog on root, SearchBox on input, ListBox on results list, and Option role on each result row.
AC12: cargo test passes with the five specified unit+kittest cases.
proof_targets: PT1: cargo test --package handshake-native-shell -- quick_switcher 2>&1 — exits 0, all tests PASS.
PT2: Real-PG integration test: open switcher in a running app with a real workspace, type 'document', verify results list populates from the backend within 2 seconds.
PT3: Screenshot of the running app with the switcher open, a query typed, and results showing kind chips + titles.
PT4: Screenshot showing the switcher closed after Escape with the workspace underneath unchanged.
PT5: cargo clippy --package handshake-native-shell -- -D warnings exits 0.
implementation_notes: Debounce: use std::time::Instant for pending_search_at; on each frame if now - pending_search_at >= 150ms and current_query != last_query_sent, fire the search and update last_query_sent; reset pending_search_at to None after firing.

Async bridge: spawn a tokio task that calls the backend HTTP endpoint; write results into an Arc<Mutex<Option<SearchResult>>> that the egui frame reads on each tick. This is the same pattern used for LoomSearchV2 in MT-264.
The recents load fires once on first open (when first_open=true); after that it is only updated optimistically on selection.
hit_key is &str concat of source_kind + ":" + ref_id — keep this as a free fn, not a method, to avoid lifetime issues with the struct.

open_target_for_hit: use serde_json::Value::get() to extract metadata fields (e.g. metadata["slug"] for user_manual_page, metadata["projection_id"] for wiki_page, metadata["symbol_entity_id"] for symbol). Emit Unsupported if metadata field is missing.
Do not hard-code workspace_id; read it from app_state.active_workspace_id: Option<String>; if None, show 'No workspace selected' and skip backend calls.
The result row layout: use ui.horizontal(|ui| { ui.label(kind_chip); ui.label(title_bold); ui.with_layout(RIGHT, |ui| { ui.label(target_label_muted); }); }); — use egui Layout combinators.
Excerpt truncation: if excerpt.len() > 80 then &excerpt[..77] + "..." else full.
Port the first_open focus trick from QuickSwitcher.tsx (window.setTimeout(..., 0)) as: if first_open { ui.memory_mut(|m| m.request_focus(search_id)); first_open = false; }
Reset query, results, selected_index, and error to defaults each time the switcher is opened (open flips from false to true).
ports_from_react: app/src/components/QuickSwitcher.tsx — full component: QUICK_SWITCHER_SOURCE_KINDS list, SOURCE_LABELS map, Props interface (workspaceId, callback handlers), useEffect for recents load, useEffect for debounced search (150ms timer), orderedResults useMemo sort, selectHit handler, keyboard onKeyDown handler, render layout

app/src/lib/api.ts — searchLoomGraph (GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search), listQuickSwitcherRecents (GET /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents?limit=20), recordQuickSwitcherRecent (POST /workspaces/{id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents), LoomGraphSearchHit type, LoomGraphSearchSourceKind type, QuickSwitcherRecent type, RecordQuickSwitcherRecentRequest type
app/src/lib/loom_search_open_target.ts — openTargetForLoomSearchHit function: maps LoomGraphSearchHit to a typed navigation target with enabled flag
binds_backend_api: POST /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/quick-switcher/recents — record visit on selection (body: { result_kind, source_kind, ref_id, title, excerpt, metadata })

hbr_obligations: HBR-INT: the five unit+kittest cases must pass; the real-PG integration test (PT2) must pass against a running backend before validator sign-off
HBR-VIS: AccessKit Dialog/SearchBox/ListBox/Option tree must be complete so swarm agents can drive navigation without screen scraping

HBR-SWARM: swarm agents must be able to open the switcher (via app_state flag), inject a query string, and read the ordered results from the accessibility tree — document this in a module-level doc comment
HBR-QUIET: async backend calls must happen on a background thread / tokio task; the egui frame thread must never block on HTTP I/O

risks: R1: If the async search result arrives mid-frame and mutates the results Vec inside the Arc<Mutex<>>, the egui borrow checker may panic if the results are also borrowed for rendering in the same frame
R2: A very fast typist may fire multiple in-flight search requests; the last-to-arrive result may not be the most recent query, causing stale result display

R3: The recents POST may fail silently (network error, backend down) while navigation still proceeds — the local recents list update would be skipped, causing recents ordering to degrade without operator feedback
R4: workspace_id may be None during early startup or after workspace switch; if the search fires before workspace_id is set, the API call will use a malformed URL
R5: Selecting a MicroTask result requires a kernel-dcc tab to exist in the active pane's tabs list; if the current pane module does not include kernel-dcc, navigation silently does nothing
R6: Accessibility tree Option IDs are computed as 0xC4_0017_0100 + row_index; if there are more than 156 results (0xFF), the ID overflows into the next block reserved for another widget
minimum_controls: MC1: Take the Arc<Mutex<>> lock with try_lock() in the rendering path; if try_lock fails (background thread is writing), skip the update this frame and re-read next frame — never hold the lock across ui.* calls
MC2: Store a search_sequence: u64 counter; increment on each dispatch; the async task returns its sequence along with the results; only accept results if returned_sequence == current search_sequence
MC3: If the recents POST fails, log the error to the recents_error field and display it in the status row (port of the React recentsError state) so the operator sees it without a crash
MC4: Guard all backend calls with: if app_state.active_workspace_id.is_none() { state.error = Some("No workspace selected".into()); return; } before spawning the async task
MC5: Before dispatching MicroTask navigation, check if kernel-dcc tab is in the active pane's tabs list; if not, add it to the pane's tabs and set activeTab to kernel-dcc before navigating
MC6: Cap AccessKit row IDs at 0xC4_0017_0100..=0xC4_0017_01FF (max 255 rows); since limit=25, this is safe; add a debug_assert!(results.len() <= 255) to catch future limit increases

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independent re-verification upholds PASS: the native quick switcher is a faithful port of the React Loom-graph switcher over the REAL PostgreSQL graph-search/recents REST contract, with off-thread HTTP (search, recents-load, AND recents-POST), a 150ms debounce + monotonic stale-sequence guard, recents-first ordering, a collision-checked AccessKit Dialog/SearchBox/ListBox/Option tree, and genuine end-to-end kittest proofs with no fakes on the required path.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC1: Mod-P (or configured chord) opens the centred switcher overlay
acceptance_summary.evidence: app.rs:3965-3970 consumes COMMAND+P (cross-platform Mod-P) via consume_key and calls toggle_quick_switcher(); distinct from the palette's COMMAND+SHIFT+P at app.rs:3946-3956. The overlay is a centred egui::Window anchored CENTER_CENTER with fixed_size [600,460], title_bar(false), collapsible(false) (quick_switcher.rs:979-986). The GO-menu seam (OpenQuickSwitcher, top_menu_bar.rs:239/439, dispatched in app.rs) also opens it. The hardcoded default matches the WORKSPACE_SETTINGS default chord 'Mod-p' (workspace_settings.rs:135-140); the configured-override branch is not wired (non-blocking, mirrors the command-palette pattern).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC2: Search input auto-focuses on open
acceptance_summary.evidence: quick_switcher.rs:1016-1019 request_focus on the first frame after (re-)open (focus_requested guard). kittest search_input_is_focused_on_open asserts switcher_search(&harness).is_focused() (test_quick_switcher.rs:194-207).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC3: Typing+150ms fires graph-search with all 9 source_kinds & limit=25 against real PG
acceptance_summary.evidence: LoomGraphSearchClient::search builds GET {base}/workspaces/{id}/loom/graph-search with q, source_kinds=QUICK_SWITCHER_SOURCE_KINDS.join(',') (all 9 kinds, quick_switcher.rs:97-107,557), and limit '25' (quick_switcher.rs:560-567). SEARCH_DEBOUNCE=150ms (line 111); SearchManager.tick fires only after now-pending_since >= 150ms (lines 738-749), proven by search_manager_fires_after_debounce_only + reanchors test. Endpoint is real PG: api/loom.rs:287 registers /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/graph-search; storage path is genuine SELECT-backed graph search.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC4: Status shows 'Searching graph...' then '{N} result(s)'
acceptance_summary.evidence: quick_switcher.rs:1024-1041 builds the status string: empty->hint, no workspace->'No workspace selected', loading->'Searching graph...', error->err, else '{n} result(s)' with singular/plural and optional recents-error note. Driven by view.loading (SearchManager.loading(), app.rs:1605).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC5: recents-first ordering by recents-list order
acceptance_summary.evidence: ordered_results stable sort_by_key on recents position (usize::MAX for non-recents) (quick_switcher.rs:407-417). ordered_results_sorts_recents_to_front_in_recents_order asserts exact recents-rank order; ordered_results_no_recents_preserves_order covers the empty-recents path.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC6: Selecting a document opens it in the active pane
acceptance_summary.evidence: target_to_tab maps Document -> (PaneType::AtelierEditor, document_id) (app.rs:1436); open_switcher_hit inserts+activates the tab on module_target_pane and sets active_pane (app.rs:1499-1513). Faithful native tab-model adaptation of the React openDocuments push. document_id_from_hit requires the KRD- prefix (quick_switcher.rs:244-265), matching the React documentIdFromLoomSearchHit.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC7: Selecting a work_packet navigates kernel-dcc + sets WP focus
acceptance_summary.evidence: target_to_tab maps WorkPacket -> (PaneType::KernelDcc, 'WP:{wp_id}') (app.rs:1441). enter_opens_hit_target_and_closes asserts the opened tab is PaneType::KernelDcc with content_id 'WP:WP-KERNEL-011' (test_quick_switcher.rs:303-313). work_packet_id extracts the id from metadata or via the boundary-anchored first_id_match scanner (quick_switcher.rs:317-325).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC8: Selecting calls POST recents and prepends returned hit_key
acceptance_summary.evidence: open_switcher_hit optimistically prepends hit_key (app.rs:1465-1468) then spawns record_recent OFF-thread on the runtime handle (app.rs:1474-1488); drive_quick_switcher drains the confirmed key and reconciles it to the front (app.rs:1556-1571). enter_opens_hit_target_and_closes asserts recents().first()=='work_packet:WP-KERNEL-011'; jump_does_not_block_on_recents_post (BlockingPost) proves the POST is dispatched off-thread (post_started true, post_finished false while the jump completes) and later reconciles.

quick_switcher.rs:931-937 returns SwitcherOutcome::Close on Escape before any open path. escape_closes_without_navigating asserts switcher closed, active_pane still None, recents empty, and the dialog node gone from the live tree (test_quick_switcher.rs:323-353).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC10: Unsupported-target rows render disabled and cannot activate
acceptance_summary.evidence: switcher_row uses ui.add_enabled(enabled, Button::selectable(...)) (quick_switcher.rs:1151-1156) so a disabled row's .clicked() is false; Enter only opens if open_target_for_hit(hit).enabled() (quick_switcher.rs:942-948); AccessKit node set_disabled() on disabled rows (lines 1168-1170). target_unsupported_disables_row unit test asserts Unsupported + !enabled + label.

emit_dialog_node (Role::Dialog, modal), emit_search_node (Role::TextInput via emit_interactive_node), emit_list_node (Role::ListBox), and per-row Role::ListBoxOption with set_selected/set_disabled (quick_switcher.rs:1161-1209). The 3 fixed ids (14..=16) are enumerated in DECLARED_IDENTITIES with the collision-free proof test (registry.rs:411-422,628-658). opening_switcher_renders_dialog_searchbox_listbox asserts all three roles in the LIVE AccessKit tree; typing_fires_graph_search_and_renders_hits asserts ListBoxOption rows.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC12: five specified unit+kittest cases pass
acceptance_summary.evidence: Met and exceeded. Unit (quick_switcher.rs tests): hit_key, source_label (all 9 + Unknown), ordered_results (recents + no-recents), open_target_for_hit branch coverage, first_id_match boundary scan, deserialize backend shape, SearchManager debounce/reanchor/stale-sequence/error, stub round-trip, truncate_excerpt. kittest (test_quick_switcher.rs): opening renders dialog/searchbox/listbox, focus-on-open, typing renders rows, enter-opens-and-closes, escape-closes, close-button, reopen-bumps-generation, jump-does-not-block. Ground truth (validator): non-#[ignore] suite is GREEN.

spec_realism.detail: Sub-rule 1 (deferred-live escape): grep of quick_switcher.rs + test_quick_switcher.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable/FIXME/XXX returned exit 1 (no matches). The QuickSwitcherTarget::Unsupported variant is a legitimate disabled-row state (port of the React enabled=false target), NOT a deferred stub of a required behavior. Sub-rule 2 (real-resource proof): the Handshake-owned resources are real PostgreSQL. api/loom.rs:287 registers GET /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/graph-search; api/loom.rs:298-299 registers GET+POST /workspaces/:workspace_id/loom/quick-switcher/recents. storage/postgres.rs:8240-8312 holds the genuine INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (with EventLedger append + tx.commit) and the SELECT ... ORDER BY selected_at DESC against table knowledge_quick_switcher_recents. The production transport default-wired in HandshakeApp::new is LoomGraphSearchClient::production (reqwest -> these endpoints, app.rs:691-693); the StubTransport/BlockingPost exist ONLY under #[cfg(test)] and the kittest harness via the explicit set_quick_switcher_transport seam. The deterministic kittests prove the seam (debounce, recents-first ordering, off-thread POST non-blocking, AccessKit tree, end-to-end jump) without a live server, and the #[cfg(feature=integration_tests)] #[ignore]'d live_backend_quick_switcher_searches_real_graph test drives the REAL LoomGraphSearchClient against the real PG endpoints (PT2). The #[ignore] gating is appropriate for a live-resource test and does NOT hide an unproven required behavior, because the non-blocking/ordering/render/navigation contract is fully proven by the green stub-backed kittests. Sub-rule 3 (self-cert): this independent review IS the certification per operator directive. No mock-only escape of a real-resource claim.

concerns_non_blocking: Contract-path artifact: the MT contract allowed_paths / 'Files to create' name src/frontend/overlays/quick_switcher.rs and src/frontend/overlays/mod.rs, but the real crate has no overlays/ module (verified: ls src/frontend/overlays returns nothing). The code lives at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/quick_switcher.rs, wired via lib.rs (pub mod quick_switcher) + app.rs, matching the established sibling-module layout of earlier MT overlays (command_palette.rs, settings_dialog.rs). This is a stale-contract path note, not a code defect; recommend the orchestrator record the actual path in the WP record.

AC1 configured-chord branch: the open chord is hardcoded COMMAND+P rather than read live from WorkspaceSettings keybindings['app.quick_switcher.open']. The default chord (Mod-p, the React default per workspace_settings.rs) is honored, so functional intent is met; reading the configured override is a follow-up if rebindable chords become a hard requirement (mirrors the command-palette pattern).

MicroTask content_id encodes as 'MT::{mt_id}' (empty wp segment) when wp_id is absent (app.rs:1444 via format!('MT:{}:{mt_id}', wp_id.unwrap_or_default())). Self-flagged accepted MINOR; ambiguous but does not break the proven WP/document/symbol paths. Worth a follow-up to encode mt_id unambiguously when wp_id is missing.

Build-output hygiene: a Handshake_Artifacts/ directory is present under src/frontend/. MT-017 touched only product source (quick_switcher.rs, app.rs, lib.rs, registry.rs) + its test file and no build/config that targets the source tree, so this is a pre-existing artifact-hygiene concern, not an MT-017-caused FAIL.
Governance paperwork: MT-017.json lifecycle.status is still PENDING and validator_verdict PENDING. Per operator directive this is NOT a blocker; this independent review is the certification.
